title: Structured Observability, Health, Profiling, Capacity, and Incidents · Program Defense
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for structured observability, health, profiling, capacity, and incidents and requires the smallest safe low-cardinality service evidence and incident dashboard with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for low-cardinality service evidence and incident dashboard. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Emit one structured request completion record with request and trace IDs, method, route pattern, status, duration, bytes, trusted peer class, and redaction.
Measure duration distributions, active requests, request and response bytes, status classes, cancellations, rejects, saturation, and dependencies with bounded attributes.
Apply current OpenTelemetry server span kind, low-cardinality route, method, status, error.type, network and server attributes, propagation, sampling, and 4xx semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
